README.md:
# Compliance & Privacy Investigation Assistant

A Vite + React + Supabase Edge Function app that helps turn de-identified investigation notes into a structured compliance/privacy investigative report.

## Important privacy note

This demo does **not** save reports in the browser or include a database write path, but investigation notes are sent to the Supabase Edge Function and then to Anthropic for analysis. Use anonymized/de-identified data only unless the production environment has been reviewed for HIPAA/privacy/security requirements and the appropriate agreements are in place.

## Local setup

```sh
npm install
cp .env.example .env
npm run dev
```

Then set your real Supabase values in `.env`.

## Supabase Edge Function secrets

Set these in Supabase, not in the frontend `.env` file:

```sh
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=your_anthropic_key
ANTHROPIC_MODEL=claude-sonnet-4-20250514
ALLOWED_ORIGINS=http://localhost:8080,https://your-domain.com
```

## Scripts

```sh
npm run dev
npm run build
npm run lint
npm run test
npm run preview
```

## Fixed in this version

- Added missing React type imports that can break TypeScript builds.
- Made the Supabase client fail clearly if env vars are missing.
- Disabled unnecessary auth session persistence because this app does not use login.
- Added frontend length validation before invoking the AI function.
- Hardened the Supabase Edge Function with method checks, JSON validation, request size limits, better CORS handling, and clearer upstream AI errors.
- Replaced Lovable-specific Playwright config imports with standard `@playwright/test` config.
- Corrected privacy/disclaimer language so it does not overpromise that data is never sent or shared.

tsconfig.app.json:
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "allowImportingTsExtensions": true,
    "isolatedModules": true,
    "jsx": "react-jsx",
    "lib": [
      "ES2020",
      "DOM",
      "DOM.Iterable"
    ],
    "module": "ESNext",
    "moduleDetection": "force",
    "moduleResolution": "bundler",
    "noEmit": true,
    "noFallthroughCasesInSwitch": false,
    "noImplicitAny": false,
    "noUnusedLocals": false,
    "noUnusedParameters": false,
    "paths": {
      "@/*": [
        "./src/*"
      ]
    },
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "strict": false,
    "target": "ES2020",
    "types": [
      "vitest/globals"
    ],
    "useDefineForClassFields": true
  },
  "include": [
    "src"
  ]
}

VITE_SUPABASE_URL="https://your-project-id.supabase.co"

# Supabase Edge Function secret, set in Supabase dashboard/secrets:
# ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="your-anthropic-api-key"
# Optional:

# ALLOWED_ORIGINS="http://localhost:8080,https://your-domain.com"

src/lib/sample-report.ts:
export const SAMPLE_REPORT_TEXT = `Case #2024-0312-EMR

Got a call from Charge Nurse A on 3/10 around 3pm. She said she noticed Employee B had Patient C's full chart open at the nurses station but Employee B was only supposed to be doing a basic lab draw on Patient C. Charge Nurse A said she asked Employee B about it and Employee B just said "oh I always pull up the whole thing, it's easier that way."

I pulled the access logs for that day. Employee B accessed Patient C's EMR at 2:15 PM. The order was just for a CBC draw — no reason to open demographics, notes, medications, imaging, none of that. But the logs show Employee B opened everything. Full chart access lasting about 12 minutes.

Interviewed Employee B on 3/12. She was cooperative. Said she's been doing it this way for years — opens the full record even for simple tasks because "the system is slow and it's faster to just open everything at once than click through tabs." She said she didn't read anything beyond what she needed for the lab order. She seemed genuine but didn't seem to understand why it was a problem.

Talked to Charge Nurse A again. She said this isn't the first time she's seen Employee B do this. Said it's "just how she works" and other nurses have noticed too. Charge Nurse A never formally reported it before because she thought it was just a workflow habit not a privacy issue.

Checked Employee B's training records. She completed the annual HIPAA/privacy training in January 2024. The training materials specifically cover minimum necessary standard. She signed the acknowledgment.

Policy ref: Our Access Policy section 4.2 says staff should only access the minimum necessary patient information to perform their assigned task. 

HR note: Employee B has been employed here 8 years, no prior disciplinary actions, good performance reviews.

Not sure if this rises to the level of a formal violation or if it's more of a retraining situation. Need guidance on whether this pattern of behavior (habitual full chart access) counts as a violation even if she says she didn't read the extra info.`;

export const SAMPLE_REPORT_FILENAME = "Investigation_Report_Case_2024-0312-EMR.docx";
